Needs Assessments Research Data Management: What Know Where Gaps?

Tips Evaluate Consumer Apps Health April 17,1:00-2:00 Mobile applications connect more people health, wellness, fitness information than ever before.
Across mobile platforms, fitness health applications some most popular most frequently downloaded.
librarians help consumers patients navigate growing field health wellness applications?
better understanding evaluate applications, librarians help their users make informed decisions about apps they choose download.

Tips Tricks Learning Data Visualization April 4:00-5:00 Data visualization health sciences help reveal insights trends that might otherwise unnoticed.
clear visualization convey more information than endless spreadsheet.
However, learning tools challenging, especially it's your first time tackling subject.
Data visualization tools, particular, have high learning curves easy overwhelmed with resources tutorials available.
This Rendezvous session will discuss tips tricks learning data visualization, with focusing tools, Tableau ArcGIS.
Nursing Liaison's Role Evidence-Based Practice April 2:00-3:00 Evidence-based practice (EBP) part healthcare today growing importance nursing practice.
increasing interest provides librarians with avenue partnering with nursing colleagues demonstrating value librarians bring organization.
This webinar will situate evidence-based practice modern healthcare describe become central nursing practice.
webinar will focus identifying roles strategies librarians employ support nurses applying evidence-based practice patient care.
Beyond Apple Day: Providing Consumer Health Information Your Library April 2:00-4:00 Sponsored SCR, this class will teach basics providing consumer health information your library, from health reference interview planning your health program, free health resources from National Library Medicine other trustworthy sources.
This class eligible hours Continuing Education credit that counted toward Consumer Health Information Specialization.
Caring Mind: Providing Mental Health Information Your Library 3:00-4:00 Responding questions involving topics mental health challenging even most experienced librarian.
Join this interactive webinar where participants will learn effectively provide mental health information their libraries.
Participants will learn about best electronic resources consult well ways improve their print collections.
Best approaches handling interactions with emotional patrons will also discussed.
Other topics covered include: bibliotherapy; assessment/testing; future mental health.
